title: INtech
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INtech, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: Americans are destroying Flock surveillance cameras
items.description: While some cities are moving to end their contracts with Flock over its links to ICE, others are taking matters into their own hands.

items.title: The US Had a Big Battery Boom Last Year
items.description: Despite Donald Trump’s unrelenting attacks on renewable energy, there’s a quiet revolution happening on US grids.

items.title: Ex-Apple team launches Acme Weather, a new take on weather forecasting
items.description: The team that sold their last app Dark Sky to Apple are back with Acme Weather, which offers alternative forecasts, rainbow and sunset alerts, and mor...

items.title: Lamborghini is the Latest Automaker to Pull the Plug on Luxury EVs
items.description: The CEO of the supercar company says demand for high-end full electric cars is “almost zero.” Could this mean Ferrari's Luce will be dead on arrival?

items.title: The Best iPad to Buy (and Some to Avoid) in 2026
items.description: We break down the current iPad lineup to help you figure out which of Apple’s tablets is best for you.

items.title: Google’s Cloud AI lead on the three frontiers of model capability
items.description: AI models are pushing against three frontiers at once: raw intelligence, response time, and a third quality you might call "extensibility."

items.title: Uber’s new autonomous vehicle division is about survival and opportunity
items.description: Uber Autonomous Solutions will see the company taking on all the tasks associated with operating a robotaxi, self-driving truck, or sidewalk delivery ...

items.title: Uncanny Valley: AI Researchers’ Resignations, Bots Hiring Humans, Evie Magazine’s Party
items.description: This episode of Uncanny Valley covers the people resigning from AI companies and the humans getting hired by AI agents. Plus, we attend a soiree throw...

items.title: Best Puffer Jacket (2026): Patagonia, Arc’teryx, REI
items.description: Our favorite down puffer jackets will keep you warm in the backcountry and around town.

items.title: Anthropic accuses Chinese AI labs of mining Claude as US debates AI chip exports
items.description: Anthropic accuses DeepSeek, Moonshot, and MiniMax of using 24,000 fake accounts to distill Claude’s AI capabilities, as U.S. officials debate export c...
